missed_actual_themes: Quarterly yield cadence and confidence in a Q4 acceleration absent European headwinds
Status of Mexico (Cozumel/Perfect Day) construction resumption after environmental pause
Whether European yield headwinds are expected to fully reverse in 2027 and by how much
Impact of higher North American airfares on NA consumer behavior and demand
overpredicted_themes: Capital returns, buybacks, leverage targets and investment-grade timing
Detailed JV/TUI contribution reconciliations and cadence
Unsecured vs ECA financing specifics, maturities and covenant flexibility
West Coast Mexico itinerary disruption quantification
Guidance conservatism and detailed Q1 beat decomposition
summary: Coverage was strong on Mediterranean booking dynamics, yield decomposition (ticket vs onboard, like-for-like vs new assets), fuel hedging sensitivity, and loyalty/card monetization. Gaps remained on quarterly yield cadence (especially Q4 confidence), Mexico construction status, the expected reversal of European headwinds into 2027, and NA airfare elasticity. Precision was moderate; many predictions were off-topic around capital allocation, JV specifics, and financing structures that did not appear in the actual Q&A.
